name: Quantum-Si Inc
business: Quantum-Si sells next-generation protein sequencing (NGPS) platforms, including the Platinum and Platinum Pro instruments, along with consumable kits and analysis software, for research use only in proteomics. The company targets researchers studying the proteome for applications such as biomarker discovery, drug development, and systems biology.

exposures.sentence: We are a life sciences company focused on proteomics research, with the mission of transforming single-molecule analysis and democratizing its use by providing researchers and clinicians access to the proteome, the set of proteins expressed within a cell.
For example, at our November 2025 Investor & Analyst Day, with the use of these new tools and processes, we showcased a roadmap that includes recognition of all 20 amino acids by the end of 2026.

catalysts.event: First shipments of Platinum Pro occurred in March 2025
catalysts.sentence: First shipments of Platinum Pro occurred in March 2025.

catalysts.event: Anticipated launch of Proteus platform by end of 2026
catalysts.sentence: most importantly, this roadmap includes the development of Proteus TM , our next-generation platform, which was announced in November 2024, and that we anticipate launching by the end of 2026.

catalysts.event: Recognition of all 20 amino acids by end of 2026
catalysts.sentence: For example, at our November 2025 Investor & Analyst Day, with the use of these new tools and processes, we showcased a roadmap that includes recognition of all 20 amino acids by the end of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Proteomics represents a $75 billion total market opportunity spanning from life science research through diagnostics.
tamClaims.sentence: According to a 2021 SVB Leerink research report, proteomics represented a $75 billion market opportunity spanning from life science research through diagnostics.

tamClaims.claim: The proteomics research market represents a $20 billion addressable market.
tamClaims.sentence: Within this, the proteomics research market represents a $20 billion addressable market, and we are focused on an initial target market of approximately $8 billion that includes protein identification (approximately $3 billion), protein expression and quantification (approximately $3 billion) and proteoforms and post translational modifications (approximately $1.5 billion).

tamClaims.claim: The initial target market is approximately $8 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: Within this, the proteomics research market represents a $20 billion addressable market, and we are focused on an initial target market of approximately $8 billion that includes protein identification (approximately $3 billion), protein expression and quantification (approximately $3 billion) and proteoforms and post translational modifications (approximately $1.5 billion).
